paper_identity.title: A Comprehensive Survey and Experimental Comparison of Graph-Based Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search

scientific_problem.problem: Evaluation bias and architectural opacity due to monolithic macro-perspective benchmarking of proximity graph algorithms.
scientific_problem.motivation: Explosive dimensionality growth of feature representations generated by deep learning model networks.
scientific_problem.gap: Absence of a standardized modular framework to evaluate isolated algorithmic sub-components under identical compilation footprints.
scientific_problem.hypothesis: Isolating graph building and search phases into a fine-grained unbundled pipeline allows the creation of optimized hybrid graph indices that break current Pareto performance limits.
scientific_problem.research_question: What are the structural performance effects of isolated candidate selection, node pruning, and enrutamiento choices inside high-dimensional index spaces?
scientific_problem.success_criteria: The creation of an engineered unbundled framework (WEAVESS) and a hybrid Optimized Algorithm (OA) that uniformly outclasses isolated state-of-the-art baselines.

methods.name: Navigable Small World (NSW)
methods.role: Baseline proximity index graph built via continuous sequential online coordinate element insertions.
methods.claimed_advantage: Logarithmic navigation hops due to long-range connection links generated during initial building cycles.
methods.limitation: High out-degree congestion hubs inside tight local coordinate spaces degrade search scalability metrics.

methods.name: Hierarchical Navigable Small World (HNSW)
methods.role: Multi-layered hierarchical routing proximity graph index structure.
methods.claimed_advantage: Strict logarithmic search scale guarantees and elite precision stability on high local intrinsic dimensions.
methods.limitation: High index construction clock times and excessive runtime operational main memory footprint requirements.

methods.name: Navigating Spreading-out Graph (NSG)
methods.role: Single-layer proximity graph index initialized via high-quality KNNG structures pruned under MRNG spatial design constraints.
methods.claimed_advantage: Ultra-low average out-degree counts, minimal memory index layout footprint, and superior throughput acceleration inside high-accuracy zones.
methods.limitation: Extended index construction durations driven by global routing lookups needed to discover early candidate neighbors.

methods.name: Optimized Algorithm (OA)
methods.role: Synthetic hybrid modular graph index constructed from unbundled pipeline evaluation components.
methods.claimed_advantage: Simultaneously achieves high search throughput (QPS), accelerated index construction efficiency, and low memory profile footings.
methods.limitation: Requires execution handling across a bifurcated, dual-stage enrutamiento logic configuration (Guided Search + Best-First Search).
